user_query:
I'm a diplomat from Neighborland preparing a briefing on potential economic cooperation with Indonesia. I have a small, somewhat messy script that summarizes bilateral trade, and I need you to do a focused code review and refactor, then generate clean, verifiable outputs for my team.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- Data file: data/trade_indonesia_neighbors.csv
- Existing scripts to review: scripts/analyze_trade.py (main), scripts/helpers.py (may or may not be used), scripts/legacy_analyzer.py (legacy)
- There is also a legacy data file at data/obsolete/trade_2019.csv

What I need:
1) Refactor the code
- Review scripts/analyze_trade.py and refactor it into scripts/refactored/analyze_trade.py.
- The refactored script must accept command-line arguments for:
  --input (path to the CSV, e.g., data/trade_indonesia_neighbors.csv)
  --partner-country (string, e.g., Neighborland)
  --sector-summary (path to write the sector-level CSV, e.g., outputs/sector_summary_neighborland.csv)
  --flow-totals (path to write the annual flow totals CSV, e.g., outputs/flow_totals_neighborland.csv)
- It should avoid hardcoding years or partner names, remove duplicated logic, and organize analysis into reusable functions.
- When run for Neighborland on the provided CSV, it must produce the two output CSVs below.

2) Compute and write required aggregates (make sure to actually run your refactored script so these files exist):
- outputs/sector_summary_neighborland.csv with columns: year, partner_country, flow, product_sector, total_value_usd, share_within_flow
  • total_value_usd: sum of value_usd for that (year, partner_country, flow, product_sector)
  • share_within_flow: total_value_usd divided by the sum of value_usd for that (year, partner_country, flow); round to 4 decimals
  • Include both export and import for Neighborland, using only rows for Neighborland.
- outputs/flow_totals_neighborland.csv with columns: year, partner_country, flow, total_value_usd
  • total_value_usd here is the sum for that (year, partner_country, flow) across all sectors.

3) Code review notes and directory audit (requires inspecting files and directories):
- Create outputs/code_review_and_dir_audit.md that includes:
  • A concise listing of all files under data/ and scripts/ (relative paths), each flagged as either "referenced by original scripts/analyze_trade.py" or "unused/legacy".
  • At least two concrete issues you found in scripts/analyze_trade.py and a short note on how your refactor addresses each.
  • A "Verification totals" section that reports the grand totals for Neighborland by year and flow (export and import) for 2022 and 2023, derived from outputs/flow_totals_neighborland.csv (these should match the sums implied by outputs/sector_summary_neighborland.csv).

Constraints:
- Use only the provided local files; no Internet or external data.
- The outputs must be reproducible by re-running scripts/refactored/analyze_trade.py with the arguments described above.
- Keep the refactor pragmatic—don’t change the dataset schema; focus on clarity, correctness of aggregates, and basic parameterization.

Deliverables to create under the workspace:
- scripts/refactored/analyze_trade.py
- outputs/sector_summary_neighborland.csv
- outputs/flow_totals_neighborland.csv
- outputs/code_review_and_dir_audit.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
